text: I opened **15 new GitHub issues** in the acme/web repository. Each issue was titled exactly "Verify: " followed by the corresponding merged pull request's title, created for every merged PR in any repository that closes a Linear issue.

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT column_name, data_type FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'github_pull_requests'"}

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input:
{"sql":"INSERT INTO github_issues (title, state, labels)
SELECT 'Verify: ' || title, 'open', 'pull-request,verification'
FROM github_pull_requests
WHERE state = 'merged'
  AND closes_linear IS NOT NULL
  AND closes_linear != ''"}

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT column_name, data_type FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'github_issues'"}

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input:
{"sql":"INSERT INTO github_issues (repo, title, state, labels)
SELECT 'acme/web', 'Verify: ' || title, 'open', 'pull-request,verification'
FROM github_pull_requests
WHERE state = 'merged'
  AND closes_linear IS NOT NULL
  AND closes_linear != '';"}

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input:
{"sql":"SELECT COUNT(*) AS created_issues
FROM github_issues
WHERE title LIKE 'Verify: %'
  AND repo = 'acme/web'"}

text: I opened **15 new GitHub issues** in the acme/web repository. Each issue was titled exactly "Verify: " followed by the corresponding merged pull request's title, created for every merged PR in any repository that closes a Linear issue.
